name: Kili Technology Data Annotation API
what_it_does: Enterprise data annotation platform with Python SDK and REST API for annotating images, text, audio, and video datasets — featuring active learning integration, automated pre-labeling, consensus quality control, and ontology management.
use_cases: Uploading assets and creating annotation projects programmatically for ML pipelines
Integrating active learning by querying uncertain predictions for human review
Exporting annotations in standard formats (COCO, Pascal VOC, YOLO) via API
Monitoring annotation quality through consensus and agreement metrics
Automating pre-labeling by submitting model predictions as initial annotations
not_for: Simple one-person annotation without team management needs
Pure time-series or sensor data annotation without media files
Budget-conscious teams — Kili targets enterprise ML organizations
best_when: An ML team needs to manage large-scale multi-modal annotation with quality control, active learning, and programmatic integration into training pipelines.
avoid_when: You need lightweight annotation for small projects — Label Studio or Roboflow is simpler.

auth.notes: API key from Kili console. Used in Python SDK and GraphQL requests. Organization-scoped. SDK simplifies auth for most workflows.

pricing.notes: Free tier for small teams and evaluation. Enterprise pricing based on users, assets, and features. Managed annotation services available. Competitive with Labelbox.

performance.rate_limits: GraphQL API has query complexity limits; bulk operations throttled

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python SDK well-documented with extensive examples. GraphQL API available for custom queries. REST API for simpler operations. Error responses include codes and messages. Webhook delivery for annotation lifecycle events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Asset upload is idempotent by external ID. Annotation exports are idempotent. Label submission overwrites previous labels — idempotent with same label data.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL API query complexity limits can be hit with deeply nested queries
Asset storage uses Kili servers by default — configure external storage for large datasets
Active learning integration requires model predictions to be in Kili's JSON format
Annotation export can be slow for large projects — use async export pattern

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2, GDPR compliant. API key auth. TLS enforced. EU data residency. External storage configuration for sensitive annotation data. Role-based access within organization.
